exercises.name: Crunches
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place hands by your ears and brace your core.
Curl your shoulders off the floor by squeezing the abs.
Lower slowly and repeat without yanking the neck.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Arm Circles
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders.
steps: Stand tall and extend both arms straight out to the sides.
Draw small controlled circles forward.
Keep shoulders relaxed and core engaged.
Reverse direction halfway through.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Overhead Arm Reaches
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders.
steps: Stand tall with arms by your sides.
Sweep one arm up and overhead in a wide arc.
Alternate arms in a smooth, controlled rhythm.
Keep your core tight and ribs down.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Arm Swings
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, chest.
steps: Stand with arms out wide at shoulder height.
Swing both arms across your chest, then back open.
Keep the movement smooth and continuous.
Breathe steadily and stay tall.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Standing Back Leg Lifts
exercises.description: Targets the glutes, hamstrings to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand tall, holding a wall or chair for balance.
Lift one leg straight back, squeezing the glute.
Keep your torso upright and avoid arching the back.
Lower with control and repeat, then switch sides.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Pull-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the back, biceps to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Grip an overhead bar with palms facing away.
Hang with arms fully extended and core tight.
Pull your chin above the bar by driving elbows down.
Lower slowly under control to full extension.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.description: Targets the calves to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Rise onto the balls of your feet as high as you can.
Pause and squeeze the calves at the top.
Lower slowly until your heels nearly touch the floor.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Lying Scissor Kicks
exercises.description: Targets the abs, hip flexors to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with legs straight and hands under your hips.
Lift both legs a few inches off the floor.
Cross one leg over the other, then switch in a scissor motion.
Keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Crab Kicks
exercises.description: Targets the glutes, core, shoulders to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Sit with hands behind you and knees bent, hips lifted.
Hold a tabletop position with hips off the floor.
Kick one leg up toward the ceiling.
Lower and alternate legs while keeping hips high.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Crunch Kicks
exercises.description: Targets the abs, hip flexors to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended and hands by your sides.
Crunch up while drawing one knee toward your chest.
Extend the leg back out as you lower your shoulders.
Alternate legs with each rep.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Reaching Crunches
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Reach both hands toward your knees as you curl up.
Squeeze the abs at the top of the movement.
Lower slowly and repeat with control.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Sit-Back Crunches
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Sit with knees bent and feet flat, hands in front.
Lean your torso back until you feel the abs engage.
Hold briefly, then curl back up to the start.
Keep the movement slow and controlled.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with arms reaching to the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Lower one arm overhead while extending the opposite leg.
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor.
Return to start and repeat on the other side.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the chest, shoulders, triceps to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Place your feet on a low step and hands on the floor.
Hold a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending the elbows.
Press back up powerfully and keep the core tight.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Diagonal Plank
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the core, shoulders, glutes.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg back.
Hold a straight, steady line without rotating the hips.
Switch sides halfway through the hold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Tricep Dips
exercises.description: Targets the triceps, shoulders to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair and grip the front edge.
Slide your hips off the seat with legs extended.
Bend your elbows to lower your hips toward the floor.
Press back up until your arms are straight.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Legs-Up Crunches
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back and raise your legs straight toward the ceiling.
Reach your hands up toward your toes.
Curl your shoulders off the floor using the abs.
Lower slowly without dropping your legs.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Floor Pulldowns
exercises.description: Targets the back, shoulders to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with arms extended overhead.
Pull your elbows down toward your ribs, squeezing the back.
Keep your core braced and lower back flat.
Reach back overhead and repeat.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Standing Toe Touch
exercises.description: Targets the hamstrings, back to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Hinge at the hips and reach down toward your toes.
Let your head and neck relax toward the floor.
Rise back up slowly, stacking the spine.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Glute Kickbacks
exercises.description: Targets the glutes, hamstrings to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Kick one leg back and up, keeping the knee bent.
Squeeze the glute at the top with small pulses.
Lower with control and switch legs.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, calves, abs.
steps: Stand tall with arms bent at your sides.
Drive one knee up toward your chest.
Quickly switch legs as if running in place.
Stay light on the balls of your feet and pump your arms.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: High Knee Kicks
exercises.description: Targets the hip flexors, quads to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand tall with arms extended in front of you.
Kick one straight leg up toward the opposite hand.
Lower and immediately switch legs.
Keep your back tall and core engaged.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Incline Push-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the chest, shoulders, triceps to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Place your hands on a sturdy elevated surface like a step or bench.
Walk your feet back to form a straight line.
Lower your chest toward the surface by bending the elbows.
Press back up to full arm extension.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Jump Rope
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the calves, shoulders.
steps: Stand tall with elbows close and hands as if holding a rope.
Make small circles with your wrists.
Hop lightly on the balls of both feet.
Keep a steady, springy rhythm.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Targets the full body to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand with feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump your feet wide while raising your arms overhead.
Jump back to bring feet together and arms down.
Keep a steady bouncing rhythm.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Knee Push-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the chest, triceps, shoulders to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Kneel and place your hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Form a straight line from head to knees.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending the elbows.
Press back up and keep the core braced.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Standing Leg Lifts
exercises.description: Targets the glutes, hip flexors to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand tall holding a wall or chair for support.
Lift one straight leg out to the front or side.
Squeeze the working muscles at the top.
Lower with control and repeat, then switch sides.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Raises
exercises.description: Targets the abs, hip flexors to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with legs straight and hands under your hips.
Keeping legs straight, lift them to vertical.
Lower slowly until just above the floor.
Keep your lower back pressed down throughout.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Targets the core, shoulders, quads to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands under your shoulders.
Drive one knee toward your chest.
Quickly switch legs as if running on the spot.
Keep your hips low and core tight.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Plank Knee Raises
exercises.description: Targets the core, shoulders to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Start in a high plank with a straight body line.
Draw one knee toward the same-side elbow.
Return the foot and switch knees.
Keep your hips steady and core engaged.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Power March
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, calves.
steps: Stand tall and march in place driving the knees up.
Pump your arms in time with your legs.
Keep a brisk, steady pace.
Stay tall through the chest and breathe rhythmically.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the chest, triceps, shoulders to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Keep a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending the elbows.
Press back up to full extension and keep the core tight.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Reverse Crunches
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and hands by your sides.
Draw your knees toward your chest, curling the hips off the floor.
Squeeze the lower abs at the top.
Lower slowly without swinging the legs.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Running
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, hamstrings, calves.
steps: Run forward or on the spot at a comfortable pace.
Land softly on the balls of your feet.
Drive your arms in sync with your stride.
Keep your breathing steady and posture tall.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Run in Place
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, calves.
steps: Stand tall and jog on the spot.
Lift your knees and stay light on your feet.
Pump your arms to keep momentum.
Maintain a steady, even rhythm.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Targets the glutes, hamstrings to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie on your back with one knee bent and the other leg extended.
Press through the planted heel to lift your hips.
Squeeze the glute at the top, keeping hips level.
Lower with control and repeat, then switch sides.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Squats
exercises.description: Targets the quads, glutes to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out.
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower down.
Keep your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
Drive through your heels to stand back up.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Narrow Squats
exercises.description: Targets the quads, glutes to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands at your chest.
Lower into a squat keeping your chest tall.
Keep your weight in your heels.
Drive up to standing and squeeze the glutes.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Standing Knee Raises
exercises.description: Targets the abs, hip flexors to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand tall with hands at your chest.
Drive one knee up toward your chest.
Lower with control and keep your torso upright.
Alternate knees with each rep.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Step-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the quads, glutes to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand facing a sturdy step or low chair.
Place one foot fully on the step.
Drive through that heel to stand up on the step.
Step down with control and alternate the leading leg.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: V-Ups
exercises.description: Targets the abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Lie flat with arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Lift your legs and torso at the same time to form a V.
Reach your hands toward your feet.
Lower slowly under control without dropping back.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, glutes.
steps: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
Slide down until your knees are bent at 90 degrees.
Keep your knees above your ankles and back pressed to the wall.
Hold the position and breathe steadily.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Standing Oblique Crunches
exercises.description: Targets the obliques, abs to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand tall with hands behind your head.
Lift one knee up and out to the side.
Crunch the same-side elbow toward that knee.
Return tall and alternate sides.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Standing Side Bends
exercises.description: Targets the obliques to build strength and control through a full range of motion.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, one hand reaching overhead.
Bend gently to the opposite side along the waist.
Feel the stretch through your obliques.
Return to center and alternate sides.
tips: Move with control instead of momentum.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Stop the set if your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the effort, inhale as you return.

exercises.name: Big Toe Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings, back.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Hinge forward and reach for your big toes.
Lengthen your spine on each inhale.
Relax your head and hold, breathing evenly.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Half Boat Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the abs, hip flexors.
steps: Sit with knees bent and feet on the floor.
Lean back slightly and lift your shins parallel to the floor.
Reach your arms forward alongside your knees.
Hold tall through the chest and breathe steadily.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Bridge Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, back.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, hip-width apart.
Press through your feet to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Roll your shoulders under and clasp your hands if comfortable.
Hold, keeping your thighs parallel, and breathe.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Anjali Mudra (Prayer Pose)
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the chest, shoulders.
steps: Sit or stand tall with a long spine.
Bring your palms together at your heart center.
Press the hands evenly and relax your shoulders.
Close your eyes and breathe slowly.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Standing Backbend
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, chest.
steps: Stand tall with hands supporting your lower back.
Inhale and lift your chest toward the ceiling.
Gently arch back, keeping your neck long.
Hold briefly and rise back up with control.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Boat Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the abs, hip flexors.
steps: Sit and lean back slightly, balancing on your sit bones.
Lift your legs so your body forms a V shape.
Extend your arms forward parallel to the floor.
Keep your chest lifted and hold steady.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Chair Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, glutes.
steps: Stand with feet together and arms raised overhead.
Bend your knees and sink your hips as if sitting in a chair.
Keep your weight in your heels and chest lifted.
Hold and breathe deeply.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Cobra Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, chest.
steps: Lie face down with hands under your shoulders.
Press lightly into your hands and lift your chest.
Keep your elbows close and shoulders down.
Lengthen through the spine and hold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Cow Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, abs.
steps: Start on all fours with wrists under shoulders.
Inhale, drop your belly and lift your chest and tailbone.
Soften your shoulders away from your ears.
Hold, then flow back to a neutral spine.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Crescent Lunge
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, hip flexors.
steps: Step one foot forward into a deep lunge.
Bend the front knee over the ankle and lift your back heel.
Raise your arms overhead and lengthen up.
Hold steady, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Crow Pose
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the arms, abs.
steps: Squat with hands on the floor shoulder-width apart.
Place your knees on the backs of your upper arms.
Lean forward and lift your feet off the floor.
Gaze ahead and balance on your hands.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Dancer Pose
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, back.
steps: Stand tall and shift weight onto one leg.
Bend the other knee and catch that foot behind you.
Extend your opposite arm forward for balance.
Kick into your hand and lift your chest, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Airplane Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, back, core.
steps: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee.
Hinge forward and extend the other leg straight behind you.
Reach your arms back alongside your body like wings.
Hold a straight line from head to heel, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Dolphin Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, core.
steps: Start on your forearms with elbows under your shoulders.
Tuck your toes and lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Press your chest gently back toward your thighs.
Keep your head relaxed and hold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Wide-Legged Forward Fold
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings, back.
steps: Stand with your feet wide apart, toes forward.
Hinge at the hips and fold your torso down.
Place your hands on the floor under your shoulders.
Let your head hang and hold, breathing evenly.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Extended Side Angle
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, obliques.
steps: From a wide stance, bend your front knee over the ankle.
Rest your front forearm on the thigh.
Reach the top arm over your ear toward the front of the room.
Lengthen one line from heel to fingertips, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Triangle Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings, obliques.
steps: Stand with feet wide and front toes pointing forward.
Reach over the front leg, then lower that hand to your shin.
Extend the top arm straight toward the ceiling.
Open your chest and gaze upward, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Forearm Stand
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, core.
steps: Place your forearms on the floor near a wall.
Lift your hips and walk your feet in.
Kick up one leg at a time to balance on your forearms.
Engage your core and hold with steady breath.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Pyramid Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings.
steps: Step one foot forward with both legs straight.
Square your hips toward the front.
Hinge at the hips and fold over the front leg.
Rest your hands on the floor or shin, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Half Lord of the Fishes
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, obliques.
steps: Sit with one leg bent and the foot crossed over the other.
Inhale and lengthen your spine tall.
Exhale and twist toward the bent knee.
Use your arm against the knee for gentle leverage, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Handstand
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, arms.
steps: Place your hands shoulder-width apart near a wall.
Kick up one leg at a time to invert.
Stack your hips over your shoulders and wrists.
Press firmly through your hands and hold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Crane Pose
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the arms, abs.
steps: Squat with your hands planted on the floor.
Place your knees high onto your upper arms.
Shift your weight forward onto your hands.
Lift your feet and balance with straight-ish arms.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Eka Pada Koundinyasana
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the arms, core.
steps: From a low squat, plant both hands on the floor.
Hook one thigh high on the same-side upper arm.
Lean forward and extend both legs out to the side.
Balance on your hands and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Standing Splits
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings, glutes.
steps: From a forward fold, place both hands on the floor.
Shift your weight into the standing leg.
Lift the other leg as high as possible behind you.
Keep your hips level and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Legs-Up-the-Wall
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, hamstrings.
steps: Sit beside a wall and swing your legs up it as you lie back.
Rest your hips close to the wall.
Let your arms relax by your sides.
Close your eyes and breathe slowly.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Lord of the Dance Pose
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, back.
steps: Balance on one leg and catch the opposite foot behind you.
Extend your free arm forward for focus.
Press your foot into your hand and lift it up and back.
Open your chest and hold, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: High Lunge
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, hip flexors.
steps: Step one foot forward and bend that knee deeply.
Keep the back leg straight with the heel lifted.
Reach your arms up overhead.
Sink your hips and hold, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Half Moon Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, core.
steps: From a lunge, reach your front hand to the floor ahead.
Shift onto the front leg and lift the back leg parallel to the floor.
Open your hips and stack your top arm to the sky.
Gaze forward or up and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Mountain Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the full body.
steps: Stand with feet together and weight evenly spread.
Lengthen your spine and roll your shoulders back.
Let your arms rest by your sides, palms forward.
Breathe steadily and stand grounded.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: King Dancer Pose
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, back.
steps: Stand tall and balance on one leg.
Catch the opposite foot with the same-side hand.
Lift the foot high and reach the other arm forward.
Deepen the backbend and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Full Boat Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the abs, hip flexors.
steps: Sit and lean back, balancing on your sit bones.
Straighten your legs up to form a sharp V.
Extend your arms forward beside your legs.
Lift your chest and hold steady.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: One-Legged Balance
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, calves.
steps: Stand tall and shift your weight onto one foot.
Place the other foot against your standing leg.
Bring your hands together overhead.
Fix your gaze on one point and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Pigeon Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, hip flexors.
steps: From all fours, bring one shin forward toward your wrists.
Extend the other leg straight back.
Square your hips and sink down evenly.
Walk your hands forward to fold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Plank Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the core, shoulders.
steps: Start on your hands with wrists under your shoulders.
Step your feet back to a straight line.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes.
Keep your neck long and hold steady.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Plough Pose
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, hamstrings.
steps: Lie on your back and lift your legs overhead.
Support your lower back with your hands.
Lower your toes toward the floor behind your head.
Keep your neck relaxed and breathe slowly.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Extended Puppy Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, back.
steps: Start on all fours with hips over your knees.
Walk your hands forward and lower your chest.
Keep your hips high over your knees.
Rest your forehead down and hold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Reclined Spinal Twist
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, obliques.
steps: Lie on your back and hug one knee to your chest.
Guide that knee across your body to the opposite side.
Extend your arms wide and gaze away from the knee.
Relax into the twist, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Reclined Staff Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the abs, hip flexors.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended.
Lift both legs a little off the floor.
Point your toes and keep your legs straight.
Press your lower back down and breathe.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Sage Twist
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, obliques.
steps: Sit with one leg extended and the other knee bent up.
Hug the bent knee and lengthen your spine.
Twist your torso toward the bent knee.
Hold the twist with steady breath, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Bend
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings, back.
steps: Sit with both legs extended in front of you.
Inhale and lengthen your spine tall.
Exhale and hinge forward over your legs.
Hold your shins or feet and relax into the fold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the core, shoulders.
steps: Start in a plank, then shift onto one hand.
Stack your feet and roll your hips open.
Reach your top arm toward the ceiling.
Hold a straight line, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Standing Side Stretch
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the obliques, back.
steps: Stand tall and reach both arms overhead.
Clasp your hands and lean gently to one side.
Feel a long stretch along your side body.
Return to center and switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Supine Leg Raise
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the abs, hip flexors.
steps: Lie on your back with arms by your sides.
Keeping legs straight, raise them toward vertical.
Pause and engage your lower belly.
Lower slowly with control and repeat.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Staff Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, core.
steps: Sit with your legs extended straight ahead.
Flex your feet and press your thighs down.
Place your hands beside your hips and lift your chest.
Lengthen the spine tall and hold.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Warrior III
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, core.
steps: Stand on one leg with a soft knee.
Hinge forward and lift the other leg straight back.
Reach your arms forward past your head.
Form a straight line from fingertips to heel, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Full Standing Splits
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the hamstrings, glutes.
steps: From a forward fold, plant both hands firmly.
Lift one leg high toward the ceiling.
Reach to hold the standing ankle if possible.
Draw your chest to the standing leg, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Seated Side Stretch
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the obliques.
steps: Sit cross-legged with a tall spine.
Plant one hand on the floor beside you.
Reach the other arm up and over toward that side.
Feel the stretch through the ribs, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Reclined Eagle Twist
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, glutes.
steps: Lie on your back and cross one thigh over the other.
Let your knees fall to one side.
Extend your arms wide for support.
Relax into the gentle twist, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Reclined Figure-Four
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, hip flexors.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent.
Cross one ankle over the opposite thigh.
Thread your hands behind the supporting thigh.
Draw the legs toward you and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Supported Bridge
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the glutes, back.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Press your feet down and lift your hips.
Slide a support under your sacrum if you have one.
Rest your weight onto the support and breathe.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Three-Legged Dog
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, hamstrings.
steps: Begin in downward-facing dog.
Press both hands firmly into the floor.
Lift one leg straight up behind you.
Keep your hips level and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Tree Pose
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, calves.
steps: Stand tall and shift your weight to one foot.
Place the other foot on your calf or inner thigh.
Raise your arms overhead like branches.
Fix your gaze and balance, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Twisted Monkey Pose
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the hip flexors, obliques.
steps: Start in a low lunge with the back knee down.
Plant one hand on the floor for support.
Bend the back knee and reach back to catch the foot.
Twist your chest open, then switch sides.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Upward-Facing Dog
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the back, chest.
steps: Lie face down with hands beside your ribs.
Press into your hands to straighten your arms.
Lift your chest and thighs off the floor.
Roll your shoulders back and gaze forward.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Upward Salute
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, back.
steps: Stand tall in mountain pose.
Sweep both arms out and up overhead.
Reach your fingertips high and lift your chest.
Gaze up gently and breathe.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Handstand Split
exercises.description: A advanced hold that builds stability and mobility through the shoulders, core.
steps: Kick up to a handstand near a wall.
Stack your hips over your shoulders.
Open your legs into a wide split.
Press through your hands and hold with control.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Tree Pose (Prayer Hands)
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, calves.
steps: Stand tall and root down through one foot.
Place the other foot on your inner thigh or calf.
Bring your palms together at your heart.
Steady your gaze and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Warrior I
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, hip flexors.
steps: Step one foot forward and bend that knee.
Angle your back foot down and ground it.
Square your hips toward the front.
Raise your arms overhead and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Warrior II
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, shoulders.
steps: From a wide stance, turn your front toes forward.
Bend the front knee over the ankle.
Open your arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
Gaze over your front hand and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Reverse Warrior
exercises.description: A intermediate hold that builds stability and mobility through the obliques, quads.
steps: Begin in Warrior II with the front knee bent.
Lower your back hand onto your rear leg.
Reach your front arm up and back overhead.
Lengthen your side body and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.

exercises.name: Warrior I (Arms Raised)
exercises.description: A beginner hold that builds stability and mobility through the quads, shoulders.
steps: Step into a lunge with the front knee bent.
Ground your back foot at an angle.
Lift your chest and reach both arms high overhead.
Draw your shoulder blades down and hold, then switch.
tips: Keep your breath slow and even.
Ease into the position—never force it.
Stop if you feel sharp or pinching pain.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and evenly through the nose.
